message:
fix(db): eliminate redundant mutex re-acquisition in coalesce and add S3-FIFO tombstone collision tests

coalesce.rs: ShardedCoalesceMap::coalesce_or_compute() previously
dropped the shard lock after determining the Leader/Joiner role, then
immediately re-acquired the same lock solely to read .len() for the
inflight count metric. This created an unnecessary contention window and
a brief period where the count could be stale. The fix captures
map.len() while the lock is still held during role assignment, returning
it as part of the (role, inflight_count) tuple and eliminating the
second lock acquisition entirely.

s3fifo.rs: Replace the simple capacity_returns_total test with two
targeted regression tests for tombstone-related edge cases:

1. s3fifo_tombstone_collision_small_to_main: Verifies that when an
   entry is promoted from the Small queue to Main, the tombstone left
   behind in Small does not cause the live Main entry to be erroneously
   evicted when the Small queue later processes the tombstone during
   eviction.

2. s3fifo_remove_then_reinsert_collision: Verifies that removing and
   re-inserting the same key (creating a tombstone + live entry in the
   Small queue) correctly skips the tombstone during eviction without
   affecting the live re-inserted entry.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
